Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
Chi siamo

Etica Soluzioni è una software house italiana attiva da oltre 15 anni, specializzata nello sviluppo e nella commercializzazione di software gestionali proprietari.

Lavoriamo in un contesto collaborativo, con team giovani e multidisciplinari, dove le idee e il contributo operativo delle persone hanno un impatto reale sui progetti.


Cosa cerchiamo

📌 Stiamo ricercando un/a Data Analyst in vista dell'ampliamento del team AI & Analytics.


La figura selezionata, dopo un periodo di formazione e affiancamento, si occuperà di:


• monitoraggio KPI e analisi delle performance dei prodotti software;

• supporto all’integrazione di soluzioni AI all’interno dei prodotti e dei processi aziendali;

• automazione e all'ottimizzazione dei processi data-driven;

• raccolta, analisi e interpretazione di dati provenienti da diverse fonti aziendali;

• sviluppo di report e dashboard.


Requisiti richiesti

• laurea triennale in Matematica, Statistica, Informatica, Ingegneria o affini;

• esperienza di minimo 4/5 anni in ruoli analoghi;

• forte interesse per il mondo AI e data analytics;

• buona conoscenza di analisi dati e statistica;

• approccio analitico, proattività e problem solving.


Plus graditi

• buona conoscenza SQL;

• buona conoscenza di Python;

• esperienza comprovata con Power BI o strumenti di Business Intelligence similari;

• familiarità con AI generativa o Machine Learning.


Cosa offriamo

• modalità di lavoro ibrida e orario flessibile;

• contratto e retribuzione commisurati all’esperienza e al potenziale della risorsa;

• CCNL Commercio, buoni pasto da 8€;

• Psicologo aziendale;

• piani di crescita e supporto alla formazione.


🕘 Orario: full time, lunedì–venerdì (9:00–18:00)

📍 Sede di lavoro: Abbiategrasso (MI), si precisa che non si intende la fermata della metro verde, ma la città.


Per candidarsi è necessario inviare un CV aggiornato.

Le candidature in linea verranno contattate dall’Ufficio HR per un primo colloquio conoscitivo

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
All’interno del team Quality, il ruolo di Quality Reporting & Controlling Analyst contribuisce al monitoraggio e all’analisi dei principali KPI di qualità e performance operative, supportando la produzione di reportistica e insight a supporto del miglioramento continuo dei processi aziendali.




Cosa farai

Ti occuperai di raccogliere, analizzare e valorizzare dati e indicatori a supporto delle decisioni strategiche e operative, in particolare:

Raccolta, integrazione e analisi di dati da fonti eterogenee (database relazionali, sistemi BI, strumenti operativi)
Produzione, certificazione e monitoraggio dei report e degli indicatori ufficiali del gruppo
Collaborazione con il team IT per ottimizzare pipeline e modelli dati
Supporto alle funzioni business nella comprensione dei dati e nell’interpretazione degli insight
Interfaccia con dipartimenti interni, Holding e sedi per analisi ad hoc strategiche, operative e di audit
Proposta di soluzioni innovative (modelli predittivi, automazioni, nuove fonti dati) per migliorare efficacia ed efficienza




Chi stiamo cercando

Laurea in discipline ingegneristiche, matematiche, statistiche o percorsi affini
3-5 anni di esperienza in ambito reporting, controlling, data analysis o quality analytics
Ottima padronanza di SQL e dei database relazionali, preferibilmente SQL Server
Conoscenza di SAP BusinessObjects per attività di reporting
Capacità di lavorare con grandi volumi di dati e sistemi complessi
Forte orientamento analitico, precisione, attenzione al dettaglio e problem solving
Capacità di interpretare e comunicare dati e insight in modo chiaro ed efficace
Ottime doti organizzative, capacità di gestione delle priorità e di teamworking
Conoscenza della lingua inglese, preferibilmente livello B1 o superiore
Costituiscono titolo preferenziale: esperienza con strumenti di data visualization (Power BI, Tableau, Qlik), modelli predittivi o esperienza nel settore logistico




Cosa offriamo

Modalità di lavoro ibrido: 3 giorni di smart working a settimana
Buoni pasto del valore di 8 € per ogni giorno lavorativo
Un efficiente servizio taxi che collega gli uffici alle stazioni più vicine

HTML:
All’interno del team Quality, il ruolo di Quality Reporting & Controlling Analyst contribuisce al monitoraggio e all’analisi dei principali KPI di qualità e performance operative, supportando la produzione di reportistica e insight a supporto del miglioramento continuo dei processi aziendali.


Cosa farai

Ti occuperai di raccogliere, analizzare e valorizzare dati e indicatori a supporto delle decisioni strategiche e operative, in particolare:

  • Raccolta, integrazione e analisi di dati da fonti eterogenee (database relazionali, sistemi BI, strumenti operativi)
  • Produzione, certificazione e monitoraggio dei report e degli indicatori ufficiali del gruppo
  • Collaborazione con il team IT per ottimizzare pipeline e modelli dati
  • Supporto alle funzioni business nella comprensione dei dati e nell’interpretazione degli insight
  • Interfaccia con dipartimenti interni, Holding e sedi per analisi ad hoc strategiche, operative e di audit
  • Proposta di soluzioni innovative (modelli predittivi, automazioni, nuove fonti dati) per migliorare efficacia ed efficienza


Chi stiamo cercando

  • Laurea in discipline ingegneristiche, matematiche, statistiche o percorsi affini
  • 3-5 anni di esperienza in ambito reporting, controlling, data analysis o quality analytics
  • Ottima padronanza di SQL e dei database relazionali, preferibilmente SQL Server
  • Conoscenza di SAP BusinessObjects per attività di reporting
  • Capacità di lavorare con grandi volumi di dati e sistemi complessi
  • Forte orientamento analitico, precisione, attenzione al dettaglio e problem solving
  • Capacità di interpretare e comunicare dati e insight in modo chiaro ed efficace
  • Ottime doti organizzative, capacità di gestione delle priorità e di teamworking
  • Conoscenza della lingua inglese, preferibilmente livello B1 o superiore
  • Costituiscono titolo preferenziale: esperienza con strumenti di data visualization (Power BI, Tableau, Qlik), modelli predittivi o esperienza nel settore logistico


Cosa offriamo

  • Modalità di lavoro ibrido: 3 giorni di smart working a settimana
  • Buoni pasto del valore di 8 € per ogni giorno lavorativo
  • Un efficiente servizio taxi che collega gli uffici alle stazioni più vicine


*Non esitare a candidarti anche se non possiedi tutti i requisiti: non sarà un algoritmo a valutare il tuo profilo, ma il nostro team di Talent Acquisition.

Title: Renewables Data Researcher Intern – Milan, Italy
Description:
Unison is a telecom and renewables infrastructure investment firm funded in partnership with Ardian - the global investment house based in Paris with over $150 billion in assets under management. Unison commenced operations in 2003 and today invests in real estate and infrastructure hosting wireless and renewables tenants in the United States and Europe. The Company is expanding rapidly and looking for talented, entrepreneurial team members to grow with us.

We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented Intern to join our team in Milan, supporting our data acquisition and optimisation work within the renewable energy sector.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone with a strong interest and foundational knowledge in renewable energy to gain hands-on experience working with real project data across Europe.

Key Responsibilities


Data entry and ongoing data quality management
Research and validation of renewable energy project information
Telephone-based validation of project and stakeholder details
Support updates to internal datasets and tracking systems


Ideal Profile


Solid understanding of renewable energy technologies, including solar, wind, biogas and related infrastructure
Basic knowledge of how energy or infrastructure projects are structured, including planning and permitting processes
Strong attention to detail and confidence working with data
Comfortable conducting research and phone-based validation work


Location


Milan, Italy


What We Offer


Hands-on experience in a fast-growing renewable energy environment
Exposure to large-scale infrastructure data and market analysis
Opportunity to develop research and data management skills in a professional setting

Title: Candidatura Spontanea (All genders welcome)
Description:
Join the movement and find new inspiration.

Kia è un’azienda in continua evoluzione e attenta a valorizzare il talento.

Se desideri proporre la tua candidatura spontanea, puoi inviare il tuo curriculum vitaee una lettera di presentazionein pochi semplici passaggi.

Per restare aggiornato sulle iniziative, i progetti e le opportunità di Kia Italia, ti invitiamo a seguirci su LinkedIn:

🔗 https://www.linkedin.com/company/kia-italia/?originalSubdomain=it

I dati forniti saranno trattati da Kia (la “Società”), in qualità di Titolare del trattamento ai sensi del Reg. (UE) 2016/679 (“GDPR”), anche tramite strumenti informatici, esclusivamente per finalità di selezione del personale e nel rispetto della normativa vigente.

I dati non saranno diffusi e potranno essere comunicati, ove necessario, ad altre società del gruppo per finalità di selezione o a società esterne specializzate che collaborano con Kia nel processo di recruiting.

Il conferimento dei dati è facoltativo, ma l’eventuale mancata fornitura e/o mancato consenso al trattamento renderà impossibile prendere in considerazione la candidatura.

Ti invitiamo a non inserire nel curriculum dati sensibili (es. origine razziale o etnica, convinzioni religiose o filosofiche, opinioni politiche, adesione ad associazioni o sindacati, dati relativi alla salute o alla vita sessuale).

Nel caso in cui tali informazioni vengano comunque inserite, è necessario allegare una dichiarazione firmata di consenso al trattamento. In assenza di tale consenso non sarà possibile procedere con la valutazione.

Kia promuove un ambiente inclusivo e garantisce pari opportunità: le candidature di entrambi i sessi sono accolte ai sensi della Legge 903/77.

Title: Fraud Data Analyst - Barcelona
Description:
Please note that this opportunity requires relocation to our Barcelona office.

About Us

Satispay began by rethinking the simple act of a payment to remove the friction from our daily routines. But we didn't stop there. Today, we are building a complete financial platform designed to empower people and concretely improve their lives.

By giving our 6 million users a clear, open path to pay, save, and invest, we are evolving into the definitive destination for every financial need.

What You'll Be Doing

As our Fraud Data Analyst, you'll be the person who secures the future of digital payments by architecting sophisticated real-time defense strategies.

Here's What Your Day-to-day Will Look Like


Architect Real-Time Strategies – You will be responsible for our real-time decisioning engine, designing and deploying logic that combines ML scores with velocity features to make millisecond decisions.
Precision Rule Development – Translate complex fraud patterns into high-performing rules, optimizing the balance between False Positive Rates (FPR) and Gross Fraud Loss.
Deconstruct Payment Fraud – Proactively hunt for emerging schemes (ATO, social engineering) and turn these insights into end-to-end mitigation strategies.
Optimize the Decisioning Stack – Partner with Product and Engineering to define analytical requirements for new features and third-party data signals.
Calibrate ML & Heuristics – Work with Data Science to operationalize machine learning models, determining how to best "wrap" model scores with heuristic rules.
Champion Frictionless UX – Analyze security measures to identify unnecessary hurdles, designing dynamic friction strategies for a seamless user experience.
Strategic Performance Reporting – Monitor rule efficacy and portfolio health, presenting data-driven recommendations to senior leadership to evolve our risk appetite.


Expect challenges, collaboration, and the freedom to bring your ideas to life in a fast-moving and supportive environment. Things change quickly here, so be ready to adapt, take initiative, and shape your role as we grow.

Who We're Looking For

We need a problem-solver who loves teamwork and gets things done. If you're curious and ready for real ownership, you'll fit in! Does this sound like you?


Experience – You have between 3 and 7 years of professional experience, specifically within the risk, payments, or credit sectors.
Technical Proficiency – You are highly skilled in SQL and Python for data analysis and strategy development.
Domain Expertise – You have a solid understanding of payment fraud dynamics and experience working with real-time decisioning environments.
Communication – You possess excellent communication skills and are fluent in English.
Nice to Have – Experience with Machine Learning, Data Science, PySpark, or GraphQL knowledge is a significant plus.


CareAbout: how we support your impact

About

We move fast, and evolution never stops. It's a fun ride, but it can be challenging. To make sure our people truly thrive, we're committed to making their lives easier, both in the office and out in the world. That's why we created CareAbout:


❤️ Health (Private insurance for you and your family, psychological support with Serenis, mental health workshops)
💰 Financial resources (Stock Option Plan, Meal vouchers, Relocation support if you're moving countries)
⚙️ Growth and development (Professional development programs, Internal mobility, Language courses with Preply)
🌱 Flexibility (Unlimited PTO, Hybrid working policy*, Flexible working hours)
👨‍👩‍👧 Family (Enhanced parental leave, Additional leave for child sickness)
We embrace three days per week in-office (Tuesday and Thursday + 1 of your choice), with the option to request extra remote time.


Equal opportunity employer

At Satispay, we're proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and inclusion, welcoming individuals of all backgrounds. This opportunity is open to everyone, regardless - for instance - of race, colour, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, and national origin. Join us in a workplace where everyone belongs!

Title: Internship Data & Market Analyst - Out of Hospital
Description:
Oxygen, nitrogen, hydrogen, and many other essential small molecules are the invisible pillars of our world and our lives. They have been at the core of the Group’s activities since its creation in 1902.

A world leader in gases, technologies and services for industry and healthcare, Air Liquide acts as the backbone of numerous economic sectors, serving 4.3 million customers and patients across 59 countries with approximately 65,000 employees.

The Group is a leader with a diversified, resilient business model and a strong local footprint across the globe. Through deep engineering expertise and technological innovation, Air Liquide provides scalable solutions that enhance industrial efficiency, accelerate decarbonization, and strengthen value chains. Strategically exposed to growth markets and megatrends, the Group accompanies major industrial and societal transformations to create long term added value and build a sustainable future.

How will you CONTRIBUTE and GROW?

Entrando a far parte del team commerciale Medgas, raccoglierai e analizzerai dati relativi al mercato Out of Hospital (OoH) con l’obiettivo di individuare le aree commerciali di maggior potenziale e supportare il business nelle valutazioni strategiche.

Obiettivo del team Out Of Hospital (OoH) è aumentare il market share nel mercato OoH. La World Business Line (WBL) ha lanciato la nuova value proposition per OoH, tra i cui assi di crescita figurano la costruzione delle Power Zone e la valutazione di segmenti e aree di sviluppo. La nuova risorsa, collaborando con il Senior Key Account Manager OoH e con i colleghi della WBL, lavorerà per costruire le aree ad alto potenziale e i segmenti di interesse per garantire gli ambiziosi obiettivi di crescita.

Contesto e obiettivi

Il mercato Out Of Hospital (OoH) include servizi di ambulanza, residenze sanitarie assistite (RSA), case di cura, studi odontoiatrici, veterinari e assistenza domiciliare (HAD). Per rispondere in modo sempre più efficace alle esigenze dei clienti è fondamentale supportare il business nello sviluppo di nuovi segmenti e nel consolidamento dei principali attori di mercato.

Quale sarà la tua missione?

Ricerca, estrazione e pulizia di dati provenienti da banche dati regionali e fonti istituzionali.

Preparazione e coordinamento dei dati e delle coordinate geografiche necessarie per individuare e mappare con precisione le aree a più alto potenziale commerciale.

Analisi dei dati e sviluppo di business case relativi alle aree commerciali individuate a supporto delle decisioni strategiche del business.

Are you a MATCH?

Laurea in discipline Economiche, Statistiche, Ingegneristiche o affini.

Ottima padronanza della suite Google e/o Office, forte attitudine all’utilizzo di tool e sistemi digitali, curiosità e proattività nell'esplorare l'applicazione di tecnologie emergenti per l'ottimizzazione dei processi e l'analisi strategica dei dati.

Spiccate capacità di analisi, sintesi, precisione e attenzione ai dettagli, approccio collaborativo e comunicazione efficace.

Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese.

At Air Liquide, we are committed to build a diverse and inclusive workplace that embraces the diversity of our employees, our customers, patients, community stakeholders and cultures across the world.

We welcome and consider applications from all qualified applicants, regardless of their background. We strongly believe a diverse organization opens up opportunities for people to express their talent, both individually and collectively and it helps foster our ability to innovate by living our fundamentals, acting for our success and creating an engaging environment in a changing world.

Title: Social Data Analyst
Description:
Ciao, sono Francesca, Strategy, Marketing & Content Director in The Next Generation Platform, e sono alla ricerca di un* Social Data Analyst con forte specializzazione in social media analytics e digital intelligence che supporti il team strategy nello sviluppo di sistemi avanzati di social reporting, performance monitoring e social listening.

Due indicazioni sulla company, sul team e sul ruolo.

Partiamo Dalla Company

Together è l’agenzia di comunicazione di The Next Generation Platform. Traduciamo strategie guidate dal significato in strategie e campagne a impatto generativo. Sviluppiamo idee che aiutano i brand a diventare agenti di cambiamento positivo, attraverso un approccio strategico e creativo unico, basato sull’essere umano.

Di cosa ci occupiamo?

Dalla discovery alla definizione di brand purpose e strategie di brand, fino alla realizzazione di campagne, progetti creativi integrati e content strategy, media strategy, reporting & optimization.

Nella Pratica

La figura che cerchiamo avrà un ruolo chiave nella progettazione e implementazione di un nuovo ecosistema di reporting e analisi dati social, contribuendo a trasformare dati e conversazioni online in insight strategici per clienti e team interni.

Social performance & reporting


Progettare e sviluppare dashboard di social performance (organico + paid)
Monitorare KPI e performance dei contenuti e delle campagne
Creare report operativi e strategici per clienti e team interni
Supportare strategy e content team nell’interpretazione dei dati



Social listening & digital intelligence


Analizzare conversazioni online attraverso tool di social listening
Costruire query di ascolto e taxonomy di analisi
Analizzare sentiment, topic, trend culturali e community signals
Trasformare i dati in insight utili per strategie di comunicazione e contenuto



Reporting infrastructure


Valutare e comparare tool di social listening e social reporting
Supportare l’implementazione o migrazione verso un nuovo sistema di reportistica
Definire standard di KPI, metric mapping e continuità storica dei dati
Supportare budgeting e valutazione dei costi delle piattaforme



Sei la persona che stiamo cercando se hai…


5 o più anni di esperienza in social data analysis o digital intelligence
Esperienza in agenzie di comunicazione, centri media o consulenza digitale
Esperienza nella costruzione di dashboard e sistemi di reporting multi-cliente
Forte capacità analitica e di sintesi
Ottima conoscenza delle principali metriche social e digital (reach, engagement, retention, video metrics, audience growth, performance paid).
Esperienza su più piattaforme come: Brandwatch, Talkwalker, Meltwater, Sprinklr, Pulsar, Emplifi. Ottima conoscenza di: Looker Studio, Funnel.io
Data visualization
Familiarità con piattaforme adv: Meta, TikTok, YouTube / Google e LinkedIn
Attitudine a trasformare dati complessi in insight chiari e azionabili
Curiosità verso evoluzione delle piattaforme social e dei tool di analisi
Attitudine alla collaborazione con team strategy, content e media



Cosa Offriamo


Contratto a tempo indeterminato full time con un range di RAL previsto tra 33.000-35.000€
Flessibilità: smart libero. La performance la misuriamo sugli obiettivi, non in ore lavorate
Buoni pasto di 8€/giorno anche quando sei in smart
Welfare su piattaforma dedicata



Iter Di Selezione


Leggeremo il tuo CV (non Europass please!);
Videocall con People&Culture
Invio Business Case
Colloquio in presenza: ti inviteremo al C32 per un colloquio e conoscerai diverse key people di Together. Si parlerà di ruolo, aspettative, culture fit e opportunità di crescita.



Se senti che questa realtà e questa opportunità sono in linea con il tuo percorso e le tue ambizioni, inviaci la tua candidatura: non vediamo l’ora di conoscerti!

Garantiamo un processo di selezione equo, aperto a tutte le persone, indipendentemente da genere, età, origine, orientamento sessuale, disabilità o qualsiasi altra caratteristica protetta dalla normativa vigente (L. 903/77 e L. 125/91)

Title: Global Analytics & Insights - Junior Analyst
Description:
Generali is one of the largest global insurance and asset management providers. Established in 1831, it is present in 50 countries in the world the Generali Group is one of the most significant players in the global insurance and the asset management industry. Generali supports each firm to innovate and grow with full investment autonomy ensuring they are set up to develop sustainable and innovative solutions. With the stability of Generali Group backing, the platform enables investors to access distinctive strategies and experts' insights.




Generali Employee Benefits (GEB) Network is now Redion, a Generali Company.




We are Redion. This is not a new company or a restructuring. This is our new global name — one brand for Europ Assistance and Generali Employee Benefits (GEB) Network, bringing together our insurance, assistance, and employee benefits services worldwide. Our vision: to be the world’s premier care partner.

Redion Employee Benefits is the global platform dedicated to the human capital of multinational corporations and, following the acquisition of Swiss Life Network earlier in 2026, the undisputed worldwide leader in employee benefits.







For more information, please visit our website www.redion.com/eb







** We are looking for 2 Junior Analysts to join our Global Analytics & Insights Team. Application must be submitted in English. **







The Junior Analyst, within the Global Analytics & Insights Team, will be responsible for consolidating and analysing financial and operational data for our Multinational Clients. This role involves collaborating with internal business groups, operations teams, and external managers to enhance the customer experience with regards to their financial results. The Junior Analyst will execute client reports, troubleshoot data anomalies, and compile concise narratives for results prior to distribution to key business partners.

You will join a fast-paced, international environment within a young and dynamic team that strongly values onboarding, knowledge sharing, and collaboration - because we believe we achieve more together. Fluency in English is essential; additional languages are considered a strong asset.




Key responsibilities (non-exhaustive list):

Receive, manage and administer data regarding pool, captive clients and prepare their annual and quarterly reports
Analyse, inform and explain data of pool and captive clients to the Account Executives and Client Development Managers in the commercial office
Assist in informing and explaining data of pool and captive clients to the internal and external auditors
Responsible for the update of the pool and captive documentation (contracts, agreements)
Manage the captive warranties (extending to the letter of credit, change of guarantee or change of level...)
Identify, suggest and coordinate any necessary developments that are required to support business needs/expansions. This will include preparation of Business Requirements documentation, coordination with internal/external stakeholders




The above list is not limitative and may be amended/adapted at any time by the Employer, at its own discretion, in accordance with the business needs.







What we are looking for candidates with

strong ability (and willingness) to work with figures
strong analytical skills and high level of computer literacy, mainly in Excel, PowerPoint and Word
proactive attitude and able to anticipate the consequences of every decision taken (this comes with time & exposure but it is definitively what the incumbent will have to show)
ability to work under pressure with tight deadlines while delivering a high-quality work
a team oriented approach but still able to work independently







Ideal background would be

1 to 3 years of experience in a role combining data management, analytics and providing insights
combined with a degree in Quantitative field (Mathematics, Actuarial Sciences, Economics and Finance or similar)
knowledge/experience of life insurance or reinsurance is definitively a major asset
















Redion Employee Benefits’ commitment for recruiting:

Redion Employee Benefits is committed to promote equal opportunities in employment. Candidates will receive equal treatment regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, marital or civil partner status, pregnancy or maternity, race, color, nationality, ethnic or national origin, religion or belief, sex or sexual orientation.

At Generali and Redion, we believe that it is our differences that make the difference. At the heart of everything we do, we value the fact that we are all human beings, unique in our own ways, bringing different cultures, lifestyles, mindsets, and preferences. Our commitment is to leverage this Diversity to create long-term value, to be innovative, sustainable, to make the difference for our people, our clients, our partners as well as our communities. We strive to promote a culture where D&I is embedded in how we work and do business every day. All of us around the world are taking actions every day to create an inclusive and accessible workplace, where every person feels empowered to take ownership, to challenge biases and lead the transformation with a human touch.




Redion Employee Benefits endeavours to contact candidates within 21 days of application. However, if you do not hear back after 3 weeks then please assume on this occasion, unfortunately, you have not been successful.




Personal data collected will be strictly used for recruitment purpose only. All unsuccessful applications will be destroyed max. 3 months after this recruitment campaign closing.




You can consult our candidate’s Privacy notice here: https://www.geb.com/privacy-notice-job-applicants

Title: Data-analist
Description:
Consultant- Data Analyst (NL) - Exsolvæ – Brussel en omgeving (op locatie)




Over Exsolvæ

Exsolvæ is een Brussels consultancybureau gespecialiseerd in Data en Artificial Intelligence. Wij begeleiden onze partners bij complexe, vaak unieke uitdagingen via consultance, audits en het ontwikkelen van op maat gemaakte oplossingen. Onze aanpak is bewust holistisch: we laten Data Engineering, Advanced Analytics en AI samenkomen, want wij geloven erin dat daar de échte impact ontstaat.

Bij Exsolvæ noemen we onze consultants Solvers; gepassioneerde professionals die met de nieuwste technologieën werken, blijven leren, kennis delen, en gedreven zijn om duurzame oplossingen met écht resultaat te leveren.




Over de rol

Als Data Analyst speel je een sleutelrol in het omzetten van data naar inzichten die het verschil maken bij strategische beslissingen. Je werkt nauw samen met business- en technische teams om sterke datamodellen te ontwerpen, krachtige dashboards te bouwen, en ervoor te zorgen dat data binnen de organisatie correct, veilig en efficiënt wordt verwerkt.




Wat ga je doen?

Datamodellen ontwerpen, ontwikkelen en optimaliseren die de business en strategie ondersteunen
Power BI dashboards bouwen en uitbouwen die complexe data omzetten naar heldere, bruikbare inzichten
Werken met grote datasets, met aandacht voor performance, schaalbaarheid en snelheid
Data-transformaties optimaliseren via Power Query, DAX en SQL best practices
Databricks inzetten voor data preparation, transformatie en modellering
Werken met data in cloudomgevingen zoals Azure en/of AWS om analyses en rapporteringen op te zetten
Data security (RlS/OLS) implementeren en beheren; de juiste toegang voor de juiste mensen
Kwantitatieve en statistische analyses uitvoeren om inzichten te genereren die onderbouwde beslissingen ondersteunen
Bijdragen aan data-standaarden, governance en best practices binnen de organisatie
Mee bouwen aan datakwaliteit, consistentie en betrouwbaarheid




Wat breng je mee?

4+ jaar ervaring als Data Analyst of in een gelijkwaardige rol
Sterke expertise in Power BI, zowel in dashboard design als in performance optimalisatie
Gevorderde kennis van SQL en DAX, met focus of efficiënte queries en berekeningen
Hands-on ervaring met Databricks
Ervaring in Azure- en/of AWS-omgevingen
Bewezen ervaring met tabular models en high-performance analytics
Solide kennis van datamodellering (star schema, dimensional modeling)
Ervaring met role-based security (RLS/OLS)
Een analytische mindset en een gestructureerde aanpak van problemen
Sterke communicatieve skills; je kan complexe data vertalen naar heldere verhalen
Vloeiend Engels en Nederlands en idealiter spreek je ook een woordje Frans (B1/B2)




Waarom Exsolvæ?

Werken aan projecten met écht impact
Een innovatieve omgeving waarin leren en kennisdeling centraal staan
Volop kansen om jezelf verder te ontwikkelen
Hybride werken; flexibel tussen kantoor en thuis
Een competitief salaris met een mooi pakket aan voordelen
Klinkt dit als een uitdaging die bij jou past? Laat het me weten en dan vertel ik je er graag meer over!

  • Sterke communicatieve skills; je kan complexe data vertalen naar heldere verhalen
  • Vloeiend Engels en Nederlands en idealiter spreek je ook een woordje Frans (B1/B2)


Waarom Exsolvæ?

  • Werken aan projecten met écht impact
  • Een innovatieve omgeving waarin leren en kennisdeling centraal staan
  • Volop kansen om jezelf verder te ontwikkelen
  • Hybride werken; flexibel tussen kantoor en thuis
  • Een competitief salaris met een mooi pakket aan voordelen
  • Klinkt dit als een uitdaging die bij jou past? Laat het me weten en dan vertel ik je er graag meer over!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At darwind, we are looking for a Data Consultant who enjoys bringing clarity to complexity and turning raw information into meaningful insights. You help organisations build strong data foundations, enabling better decision‑making and long‑term value creation. You will work in an environment that values autonomy, responsibility and professional growth.




What you will do

In this role, you support end‑to‑end data initiatives across business and technical teams. You will:

Collect, clean and transform data from various sources.
Build dashboards and visualisations using Power BI, Tableau or Excel.
Write and optimise SQL queries for reporting and analysis.
Support or develop data pipelines (ETL/ELT) and contribute to cloud‑based data environments.
Model datasets and design efficient, scalable data structures.
Identify patterns, trends and insights that support business decisions.
Collaborate with analysts, IT teams and business stakeholders.
Improve data quality, documentation and data governance practices.
Present findings clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.




Who you are

You bring structure, analytical thinking and clear communication. You also have:

A Master’s degree.
At least three years of experience in data management or a similar field.
Strong knowledge of SQL and at least one analytics or visualisation tool (Power BI, Tableau or Excel).
Understanding of data modelling, data structures and relational databases.
The ability to translate complex data into clear business insights.
A proactive, accurate and curious mindset.
Experience in cloud environments is considered an advantage.
Fluency in Dutch, with proficiency in French as a plus.




What we offer

We provide an environment where impact, ownership and development are central.

Competitive benefits package

Hospitalization insurance
Group insurance
Meal allowance
Your choice between a company car with EU fuel card or the federal mobility budget




Flexibility and work‑life balance

Hybrid work possibilities
Flexible working hours
Projects aligned with your skills and ambitions




Learning and growth

Continuous coaching
Technical and soft‑skills training
Opportunities to grow across different domains and technologies




Culture

A human, transparent and collaborative environment
Real ownership and influence on the projects you contribute to
A team that values initiative, authenticity and open communication




Ready for your next step?

If you want to work on projects where people, impact and growth truly matter, we would be happy to meet you.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Can’t wait to make an impact on the world? You’re not alone. Join us in driving progress in the working world and beyond.

Your journey with us

The Tax Data & Process Team is looking for an experienced consultant to strengthen their team (Data2insight) in a wide range of analytics projects related to strategy, operations, and financial performance within the Tax landscape.

You will take ownership of client workstreams, shape engagement approaches, and act as a trusted advisor on how to turn fragmented tax data into a strategic asset. You will gain exposure to multiple industries with a chance to choose a focus and help to shape Deloitte market offerings. 

Under the direction of a Manager or Lead, you will be:


Managing tax data readiness assessments
Designing end-to-end tax data journeys and identifying control gaps and automation opportunities
Architecting data transformation and migration approaches
Building, configuring and analyzing tax data sets to be used for modeling, clustering, segmentation, and data mining
Delivering analytical insights
Using a variety of technologies for process analysis and design and data analytics
Developing tax dashboard and insight layers that connect operations data to strategic decision-making
Evaluating and recommending AI tools and platforms for tax data analysis and compliance monitoring
Contributing to the development of AI-powered tax insights and advisory offerings
Mentoring junior team members and contributing to offering development, thought leadership, and go-to market strategy.


Let's Talk About You


You have 2 to 5 years of professional experience
Relevant experience in Data Science within corporate Finance and/ or Accounting.
You are a team player with strong communication skills with the ability to tailor your presentation style to different levels of the organization
You have an intellectual curiosity in combination with outstanding analytical skills
You have hands-on experience with data analysis, ETL processes, reconciliation logic, BI/Dashboarding tools etc.
You have a genuine interest / passion in tax technology, regulatory change and the evolving role of data in tax
Familiarity with AI/ML concepts and their application in finance and tax
Understanding of data governance, quality, and ethics in AI-driven solutions
You are structured and process minded
You have a true willingness and ability to work as part of an integrated, enthusiastic and multidisciplinary team
You’re open to work in an international environment and to work abroad if needed
You’re fluent in English. Dutch and/or French is a plus


As a member of our Tax Technology Consulting (TTC) team, you will be part of a team that consists of people with diverse backgrounds. Some of us are data scientists, others are SAP system specialists and others are finance/tax process generalists. What unites us is our mission to solve complex finance/tax problems focusing on processes and data supported with cutting edge technology. Through this we unlock value to our client’s tax teams by bringing efficiencies in their day-to-day processes.

Who is Deloitte?

We provide industry-leading audit and assurance, tax and legal, consulting and related services. We are committed to driving innovation across offerings to help our clients address their challenges, while giving our professionals opportunities to learn and grow in this era of transformation.

In Belgium, +5000 dedicated professionals active in +10 offices, take great pride in bringing multidisciplinary expertise to a wide variety of clients, from national and international companies, small, fast-growing and large organizations to public institutions and governmental authorities.

Why Deloitte?

Be the true you! We foster diversity and inclusion and encourage you to bring your authentic self to work. Explore, question and collaborate while building a career that inspires and energises you.

Never stop growing! Diversity of thought makes us stronger. At Deloitte, we tailor a personalized learning experience, offering you the opportunity to grow at your own pace and achieve maximum impact.

We practice what we preach! As a Purpose-led organisation, at the heart of everything we do is a set of timeless principles and unifying values .

Life looks different for each of us, so we created a varied benefits package that you can tap into:


My Benefits My Choice, a flexible rewards plan tailored to your lifestyle and priorities
Sustainable transport options offered by Mobility@Deloitte
Flexible work arrangements for all and initiatives supported by Parents & Caregivers @Deloitte
Wellbeing tips and activities powered by Energise@Deloitte
Topped off with other health benefits and insurance opportunities


Empowering our employees with flexible work arrangements remains essential in today's reality:


Hybrid workplace: combination of home office and on-site (+10 offices in Belgium or client's premises).
Part-time employment: all our jobs are open to full-time or part-time work under a 90% or 80% regime.


Join us to make an impact together! Apply now!

Title: Data Analyst / Data Visualisation
Description:
Data Analyst / Data Visualisation Consultant

Who We Are

NORRIQ is a leading international consulting firm headquartered in Denmark and Belgium, with 350+ consultants driving business transformation through technology. Our Financial Services practice includes 100+ specialists supporting banks, insurers, and financial institutions in navigating regulatory challenges, embracing emerging technologies, and meeting evolving client demands. We prioritize growth, innovation, and excellence, offering abundant opportunities for professional development and collaboration.

The Opportunity

NORRIQ is seeking to grow its Data Analytics and Artificial Intelligence team with additional Data Analyst / Data Visualisation Consultants to improve the business performance of financial institutions. You will help our clients to optimize the value they can get from their internal and external data, through improved business performance monitoring and enhanced reporting quality and readability. Join us to work on impactful projects that shape the future of finance.

Responsibilities


Collecting and interpreting internal and external data about customer behaviour via the digital platforms (Web and mobile) and their digital transactions with the bank.
Translate data insight into actionable recommendations in order to coordinate the business needs around data and improve the bank’s digital and marketing strategy and optimize future campaigns.
Collect, analyze, and interpret large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and correlations
Develop and maintain complex data models, databases, data warehouses and data marts .
Create data visualizations and reports to communicate insights to stakeholders.
Perform data quality checks and ensure data integrity, develop and implement data governance policies and procedures
Stay up-to-date with industry trends and emerging technologies in data analysis and visualization


Your Profile


Master’s degree (or equivalent work experience) in mathematics or statistics, business intelligence, computer engineering, economy.
1-3 years of experience in data projects, preferably within the financial services industry.
Technical Expertise & Tooling: SQL (mandatory), SAS (nice to have), Python & R (to support our AI team), Reporting & Visualisation tools (Power BI, Tableau, Adobe Analytics, ...),.
ETL Processes: Affinity with ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes, including an understanding of data preparation, cleansing, and integration from multiple sources.
Analytical Skills: Strong problem-solving and analytical abilities to translate complex data into actionable insights.
Business Analysis Skills: Experience in requirements gathering, process design and developing action plans.
AI knowledge: AI knowledge & interest to connect easily with our data scientist and machine learning engineers.
Communication & Collaboration: Excellent communication skills; ability to work well in cross-functional teams and engage effectively with stakeholders.
Languages: Fluent in English and native in French or Dutch.


What We Offer


Dynamic Work Environment: High-impact projects with leading financial institutions in a fast-growing consulting firm.
Career Development: Continuous learning and growth opportunities to advance your career.
Collaborative Culture: A supportive, innovative team environment that encourages new ideas and professional development.
Global Exposure: International experience working on cross-border projects that shape the financial services industry.


Why Join Us?

At NORRIQ, we are shaping the future of finance through transformation and innovation. If you're passionate about improving financial processes, driving change, and delivering value to clients, this is the perfect opportunity for you.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Freelance contract




Key Responsibilities:




1. Business Requirements Elicitation: The ideal candidate will collaborate closely with business stakeholders in Supply Chain to thoroughly understand requirements, define the associated business value, and precisely identify the data needed for business dashboards and self-service datasets.

2. Requirement Prioritization: They will prioritize business requirements and dashboard features to ensure maximum business impact and alignment with strategic objectives.

3. Ensure Requirement with Data Availability: The candidate will partner with Data/Business Analysts and SAP Functional consultants to identify required data fields, validate data sources (especially within SAP S/4HANA), and ensure high data quality and availability for reporting.

4. Design & User Experience: Designing professional dashboard mock-ups and wireframes with a focus on intuitive, user-friendly design and ease-of-use (UX/UI) will be a key aspect of their role.

5. Solution Development & Delivery: They will work alongside Analytical Engineers to facilitate the development, testing, and deployment of high-value dashboards and self-service datasets.

6. Provide Test Cases: The candidate will define comprehensive test cases and coordinate User Acceptance Testing (UAT) to ensure the delivered solution meets all specified business requirements.




Qualifications:

- Experience: A minimum of 3-5 years of experience as a Business Analyst within the supply chain domain.

- Technical Acumen: Strong proficiency in SQL for data querying and analysis.

- SAP Experience: Prior working experience with SAP S/4HANA (MM, SD) is a significant advantage.

- Communication & Analysis: Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with a proven ability to gather complex business requirements and accurately translate them into technical data needs.

- Data Visualization: Demonstrated ability to translate complex business use cases and functional requirements into effective data visualization prototypes.

- Documentation: Proficiency in creating detailed requirements documents, user stories, use cases, and functional specifications.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
You will join the Technology & Partnership Reporting team. Our team plays a key role in supporting senior management's day-to-day decision-making by delivering timely, accurate, and insightful reporting on technology initiatives and partner performance. We work closely with internal technology teams and external partners to align on reporting priorities, track performance, and surface insights that drive action, helping shape how Telenet delivers on its technology and partnership strategy.




Your tasks as a Data Analyst

You develop curiosity and a deep understanding of your domain's business context and processes.
You understand the business context and processes underlying business requests and reformulate them into clear insights questions.
You collect, query, clean, validate, and manipulate data from different sources to support reporting and analytical needs.
You define, build, and automate reporting dashboards relevant to the technology and partnership topics you are working on.
You develop a proactive partnership with your stakeholders, anticipating their needs and bringing data-driven recommendations to the table.
You perform deep-dive analyses on specific insights requests that lead to actionable recommendations for business and technology colleagues.
You contribute to optimizing reporting processes, tools, and methodologies, including the handling of ad-hoc business requests
You take an active role in the community of data professionals within Telenet, through sharing sessions, inspiration, and coaching.




Our ideal Data Analyst

You have strong analytical skills.
You have a hands-on mentality to really dive into the data and find the answers.
You have a constructive mindset and can collaborate effectively with colleagues from various teams, both from a business and a more analytical background.
You can manage an analytics initiative end to end, including strong communication & presentation skills and stakeholder management.
You have experience in reporting and a solid understanding of performance measurement.
You have solid knowledge of one of the main dashboard tools (Qlik Sense, Power BI, or Tableau); previous Qlik Sense experience is a strong plus, as it is our primary reporting tool.
You have exceptional knowledge of SQL. Experience with Snowflake is a nice to have.
You have an interest in technology, IT, and operational processes
You have a relevant degree and experience:
Master's degree in a quantitative field (business engineering, TEW, computer science, information management, etc.)
Min. 3 years work experience.
Fluent in English. Knowledge of Dutch or French is a bonus.




Our offer to you

At Telenet group, you get more than just an attractive gross salary. We strongly believe in results and ownership: we give trust but also expect commitment and impact. What you achieve truly matters.

In addition to a thirteenth-month salary and holiday pay, you can count on an extensive package of extra benefits that supports you in your daily life as well as at work.

An overview of our benefits:

Mobility budget: compose your own mobility plan, you can opt for a company car or alternative mobility solutions
Meal vouchers and eco vouchers
Mobile phone, mobile subscription, laptop, and employee discounts
Collective bonus and individual bonus (aligned with your performance)
Hospitalization insurance (including an ambulatory plan) and group insurance
20 days of paid leave and 12 compensatory days off




We value your ongoing development, investing in growth via our internal learning platform, coaching, and on-the-job training. You will have opportunities to keep learning and further strengthen yourself in your role.

We also prefer working closely together. We believe strong teams form by frequent interactions and quick transitions. To facilitate a smooth onboarding and rapid integration for new employees, we expect you to be present in the office at least three days a week during the first six months. On other days, you can work remotely from home.

What happens after you apply?

Do you see a match with our position? Then we look forward to receiving your application. We always get back to you. When there’s a match, we will reach out to you to get acquainted and explain the next steps in our selection process.

At Telenet group, you get more than just an attractive gross salary. We strongly believe in results and ownership: we give trust but also expect commitment and impact. What you achieve truly matters.

In addition to a thirteenth-month salary and holiday pay, you can count on an extensive package of extra benefits that supports you in your daily life as well as at work.

An overview of our benefits:

  • Mobility budget: compose your own mobility plan, you can opt for a company car or alternative mobility solutions
  • Meal vouchers and eco vouchers
  • Mobile phone, mobile subscription, laptop, and employee discounts
  • Collective bonus and individual bonus (aligned with your performance)
  • Hospitalization insurance (including an ambulatory plan) and group insurance
  • 20 days of paid leave and 12 compensatory days off


We value your ongoing development, investing in growth via our internal learning platform, coaching, and on-the-job training. You will have opportunities to keep learning and further strengthen yourself in your role.

We also prefer working closely together. We believe strong teams form by frequent interactions and quick transitions. To facilitate a smooth onboarding and rapid integration for new employees, we expect you to be present in the office at least three days a week during the first six months. On other days, you can work remotely from home.

What happens after you apply?

Do you see a match with our position? Then we look forward to receiving your application. We always get back to you. When there’s a match, we will reach out to you to get acquainted and explain the next steps in our selection process.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

The Role:

Our EV Volumes (EVV) Content team is responsible for the research, procurement, collection, analysis and presentation of our EV data and the updates to our vast database. The Analyst is supporting the ongoing collection, maintenance, and quality control of relevant global EV data using our proprietary systems and databases. The Data Analyst works closely with the Data Managers to carry out the activities necessary to deliver the data used by our customer.

Responsibilities


Manage the current sources of data, ensuring the timely receipt of data deliveries. Transform the data into a consistent format, capturing it within our databases in a timely manner to meet publication deadlines. Verify the accuracy of reports and assist in correcting any inaccuracies in the data.
Analyse, understand, communicate and support the resolution of data-related issues as they arise.
Finalize and deliver datasets, ensuring the highest standards of data integrity. Guarantee all publication deadlines are met through quality control and efficient data processing.
Develop and automate data production processes using additional tools. Creating scripts to improve data collection, quality assurance and processing efficiency.
Conduct market research and analysis, on all EV related topics, e.g. worldwide electrification trends, OEM and cell-supplier plans and future models.
Support the documentation & improvement of content processes at EVV.
Cooperate in quality checking the data to ensure the highest integrity and data standards are met, identifying and correcting errors prior to publication.
Support and train new colleagues in EVV data production processes.
Support on projects or assist with other activities, as needed by the Senior Managers or Executive Management team.


What You’ll Be Doing In This Role

We are looking for a candidate with a university degree in Business Administration, Engineering, Economics, Statistics/Mathematics, or an equivalent qualification, or relevant professional experience. The ideal profile will have strong technical capabilities, including advanced Excel skills (formulas, pivot tables, Power Query and macros), advanced Python for data cleaning, automation and scripting, as well as intermediate to advanced knowledge of SQL or database querying. Experience with BI tools such as Tableau at an intermediate level is also required.

A solid understanding of the automotive industry, particularly EV market developments, would be highly advantageous. We are seeking someone with strong analytical and problem-solving skills, a highly numerate mindset, and the ability to interpret complex datasets and translate them into clear, actionable insights. Excellent organisational and time management skills are essential, with the ability to consistently meet tight deadlines.

Fluency in English (written and spoken) is required, along with strong communication skills, with the ability to convey information clearly and in a structured way.

The successful candidate will be detail-oriented, highly accurate, proactive, self-motivated, and driven by a continuous improvement mindset.

From a behavioural perspective, we are looking for an individual who is objective and data-driven, a collaborative team player, demonstrates high integrity and professional standards, and is adaptable and solution-oriented.

Company Mission

Our mission in the market we serve is clear. To power every auto-related decision through proprietary data, advanced analytics, deep industry expertise, and seamless workflows that connect insight to action.

Our Values

We POWER Our Customer's Success

We are Innovative, Collaborative and Grounded in Data

We Make Things Easy

We Get It Done

We Start with Trust & Prove it Everyday

JD Power is committed to employing a diverse workforce. Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or disability.

Should you require accommodations during the recruitment and selection process, please reach out to tarecruitment@jdpa.com.

JD Power does not disclose your personal data to unauthorized third parties. However, as a global corporation consisting of multiple affiliated companies in various countries, JD Power has international sites and JD Power uses resources located throughout the world. JD Power may from time to time also use third parties to act on JD Power’s behalf. You agree to the fact that to the extent necessary your personal data may be transferred and/or disclosed to any company within JD Power group of companies as well as to third parties acting on JD Power’s behalf, including also transfers to servers and databases outside the country where you provided JD Power with your personal data. Such transfers may include for example transfers and/or disclosures outside the European Economic Area and in the United States of America. If you are a California or United Kingdom resident, additional disclosures about the information we collect and how we use that information can be found by clicking here.

To all recruitment agencies: JD Power does not accept unsolicited agency resumes and we are not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Title: Shopper Insights & Data Analysis Intern (AfH RTM)
Description:
Step into a world where data meets real impact.

Join Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP) and bring your analytical mindset, curiosity and business sense to a company that’s always evolving.

About The Internship

The Away From Home (AfH) – RTM team is looking for an enthusiastic intern to join us in Anderlecht from June onwards for a 3 to 4-month internship (extension possible).

During this internship, you will support the team in collecting and analysing shopper and commercial data, directly contributing to the preparation of the strategic plan 2027 (ABP).

You will work on real business challenges, helping the team better understand shopper behaviour and translate insights into strategic recommendations.

What You’ll Do


Collect shopper insights across DI/DC and Cash & Carry channels (sell-out data, interviews, shop visits)
Analyse shopper behaviour in drink centers (promotions, activations, pricing, etc.) using qualitative and quantitative data
Support the development of internal data tools (e.g. spill-over WHS/BWS analysis)
Translate data into actionable insights to support strategic decision-making
Contribute to the preparation of the ABP 2027


Who You Are


Student in Economics, Business, Data or similar field
Strong analytical mindset with good Excel skills
Hands-on, proactive and able to work independently
Structured, detail-oriented and curious
Strong communication skills and positive mindset
Flexible, motivated and eager to learn
Fluent in Dutch, French and English


Why join CCEP?

We join for the brands. We stay for the people.

At CCEP, you’ll be part of a fast-paced and supportive environment where you are encouraged to take ownership, bring ideas and grow.

As an intern, you will have a direct impact on a key strategic project (ABP 2027) and gain hands-on experience in data analysis and commercial strategy.

About CCEP

Coca-Cola Europacific Partners is one of the world’s leading consumer goods companies and the largest Coca-Cola bottler globally.

In Belux, around 2,000 colleagues contribute across production, logistics, sales and headquarters, managing the full value chain locally and delivering excellence every day.

Our recruitment vision

At CCEP, everyone is welcome. We value skills, potential and motivation, regardless of background.

For GDPR reasons, please apply via our official platform.

Questions? InternshipsBelux@ccep.com

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow to profoundly impact health for humanity.

The MedTech Supply Chain organization, specifically the Principal Operations COE, is looking for a Finance Analyst to join the Global Intercompany Pricing Team (ICP). This is a hybrid mix of a finance and data profile. It is not a traditional accounting or reporting role; instead, you will focus on data setups, data consolidation, transfer pricing, master data, and process improvements. Operating in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, you will serve as the backbone of the supply chain team, ensuring the robust delivery of products through regional distribution centers by setting up and optimizing ICPs.




Location: Diegem, Belgium (Hybrid: 3 days on-site at the Diegem office, 2 days remote).

Project duration: ASAP (Target start: June 2026) until 31/08/2027.

Contract type: Freelance or Consultancy (Full-time).

Language skills: Fluent in English (Minimum C1 proficiency level, both spoken and written).




The primary responsibilities for this role include, but are not limited to:

Systems & data governance: Own end-to-end systems governance. Build, maintain, and continuously improve platforms, workflows, data setups, and handoffs.
ERP execution: Execute core master data and pricing setup activities across multiple ERP systems (JD Edwards 8.12, SAP ERP, S/4HANA) accurately and efficiently.
Cross-functional collaboration: Partner closely with diverse global stakeholders (Tax Planning, Customer Service, Supply Chain Planning, NPI, Product Management) to enable seamless ICP calculation and ensure tax models are correctly applied.
Year-End support: Support annual year-end outbound/inbound and standard-cost loads across ERPs. Lead the year-end kits calculation and remain fully available to support the team during this critical closing period.
Process optimization & change: Analyze data, validate process logic, and develop workflows for large-scale testing. Maintain a mindset focused on continuous improvement and driving change rather than just executing tasks.
Compliance & controls: Coordinate the collection and retention of approvals for standard-cost and ICP changes, ensuring SOX controls are strictly enforced and audit requirements are met.
SLA & order fulfillment: Ensure base business and ad-hoc requests are completed within agreed SLAs, executing preventive controls to avoid any disruption to order fulfillment.




Experience & education:

Experience: Minimum of 3–5 years of relevant professional experience, preferably in the MedTech, pharmaceutical, or life sciences industry.
Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent, preferably in Business Administration, Finance, Information Systems, or IT.
Geographical requirement: Candidates must be based in Belgium.

Technical Skills:

Data & automation: Advanced proficiency in Excel. Proven experience in data analytics, data consolidation, dashboarding, and reporting (preferably using Power BI and SQL). Experience with Alteryx or other automation tools is a strong plus.
ERP literacy: Solid IT literacy with hands-on experience in JD Edwards and SAP ERP systems. Knowledge of SAP S/4HANA is a distinct advantage.
Project experience: Past experience as a project lead, product owner, or core project team member is a strong plus.

Soft Skills & Competencies:

Analytical & critical mindset: High level of accuracy, strong attention to detail, and a heavily analytical approach to managing large volumes of complex data.
Adaptability: Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment, manage ad-hoc requests, and comfortably navigate continuous change and "grey zones".
Cross-Cultural partnering: A proven track record of effective cross-cultural business partnering and a collaborative, team-player mindset.
Autonomy & ownership: Highly structured, proactive, and energetic, with the ability to work autonomously and manage multiple priorities.
Communication: Strong communication skills with the ability to frame, interpret, and present complex situations into practical, workable solutions.

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Job Title: Data Analytics Intern (Remote)

Company: Stealth (Confidential)




About the Role

We are hiring on behalf of a fast-growing Stealth Startup looking for a curious and detail-oriented Data Analytics Intern. This is an exciting opportunity to work closely with founding teams and gain hands-on experience in extracting insights, building dashboards, and driving data-backed decision-making in a high-impact environment.




Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, and analyze structured and unstructured data
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends and patterns
Build and maintain dashboards and reports for business insights
Write efficient SQL queries to extract and manipulate data
Assist in defining KPIs and metrics for business performance
Collaborate with product, marketing, and business teams
Present insights in a clear and actionable manner




Required Qualifications

Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or related field

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Senior Consultant Reporting & Analytics

Location: Anderlecht, Belgium

Duration: ASAP - End of 2026 with Extension possible

Remote Work: Hybrid mode 1 - 2 days onsite per week

Languages: English




Job Description - Roles and Responsibilities

Context:

The consultant will play a key role in shaping and delivering the reporting and analytics landscape, combining strategic vision with hands-on execution. This includes driving modernization of reporting capabilities, integrating Generative AI into analytics (e.g., Power BI Copilot), and supporting ongoing reporting initiatives across the portfolio.
The role requires a strong ability to connect business needs, data architecture, and advanced analytics capabilities, while managing active reporting projects.

Key Responsibilities:

1. Reporting Strategy & Transformation

Support the definition and evolution of the reporting and analytics strategy
Assess and optimize the current reporting landscape (tools, processes, governance)
Identify opportunities to enhance reporting through automation, standardization, and AI

2. Generative AI in Reporting

Drive the integration of Generative AI capabilities into reporting solutions (e.g., Power BI Copilot or similar tools)
Explore and implement use cases to enhance insights generation, user interaction, and decision-making
Provide guidance on best practices and scalability of AI-enabled reporting

3. Solution Architecture & Data Landscape

Contribute to the architecture design of reporting solutions
Ensure alignment between reporting tools, data platforms, and enterprise architecture
Collaborate with data engineering teams to ensure robust data pipelines

4. Data & Technology Expertise

· Work hands-on with:

o Power BI (advanced reporting, modeling, dashboards) & Qlik Sense

o Data warehousing solutions (e.g., Snowflake)

o ETL tools (e.g., Informatica or similar)

o Ensure data quality, scalability, and performance of reporting solutions

5. Project & Portfolio Management

Lead or support day-to-day management of reporting projects
Coordinate stakeholders across business and IT (EU, US, global teams)
Provide visibility on progress, risks, and priorities
Contribute to portfolio management of reporting and analytics initiatives

6. Stakeholder Engagement

Act as a bridge between business teams, data teams, and leadership
Translate business needs into actionable reporting solutions
Support alignment across regions and functions

Required Experience & Skills:

Proven experience in designing and managing reporting landscapes
Experience integrating or scaling Generative AI in analytics/reporting environments
Strong track record in delivering complex reporting or data transformation projects

Technical Skills:

Advanced expertise in Power BI (both strategic and hands-on)
Strong understanding of data warehousing (e.g., Snowflake or similar)
Experience with ETL tools (e.g., Informatica or equivalent)
Knowledge of modern data architectures and reporting ecosystems

Other Skills:

Strong strategic thinking with hands-on delivery capability
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
Ability to operate in a complex, international environment

Profile:

Strategic thinker with the ability to translate vision into execution
Comfortable working both at architecture level and detailed implementation level
Proactive, structured, and able to manage multiple priorities
Strong collaborator with a global and cross-functional mindset

Title: DATA ANALYST (H/F/X)
Description:
Titre de la fonction

DATA ANALYST (H/F/X)

Type de contrat

CDI

Régime de travail

Temps plein

TRAVAILLER CHEZ SIBELGA, ÇA BOOSTE MON ÉNERGIE !

Sibelga est le gestionnaire du réseau de distribution d'électricité et de gaz naturel dans la Région de Bruxelles-Capitale. Nous assurons un approvisionnement énergétique fiable et durable à nos clients. Nous visons l'innovation et l'efficacité dans nos services.

QUEL SERVICE REJOIGNEZ VOUS?

Digital Business Solutions (DBS), le département informatique de Sibelga, soutient l’ensemble des activités « métier » de Sibelga. Il leur permet de se dérouler efficacement et de se développer. Il veille à doter Sibelga d’un système informatique autonome, aligné sur l’évolution de ses besoins et des technologies.

L’équipe « Data Management & Analytics » développe et maintient une plateforme de données (incluant un datawarehouse) dont l’objectif est de produire de l'information à valeur ajoutée destinée à soutenir la prise de décisions, le pilotage des activités et la gestion de la performance, ainsi qu'à alimenter le reporting officiel.

VOTRE JOB

En tant que Data Analyst, vous êtes responsable des tâches suivantes :


Vous recueillez et comprenez les besoins en reporting managérial et opérationnel exprimés par le business et les Business Process Analystes (BPA).
Vous rédigez et structurez les spécifications business et les analyses fonctionnelles (business rules) en collaboration avec le BPA et les IT Analystes.
Vous prototypez les business rules pour s’assurer de leur validité en SAS Enterprise Guide.
Vous analysez et profilez les données issues des systèmes opérationnels pour valider la qualité de ces dernières.
Vous définissez avec les BPA un glossaire permettant d’aligner l’ensemble des utilisateurs sur les concepts et leur définition utilisés en BI.
Vous modélisez logiquement le modèle d’entreprise DWH et le modèle BI (DataMart).
Vous contribuez à la mise en place des solutions de reporting en SAS VA.
Vous testez et documentez les solutions mises en œuvre en amont des Business Acceptance Tests (BAT).
Vous supportez le business dans la rédaction des tests scenarios (sur base des rapports et des business rules), la définition des test cases et l’exécution des BAT.
Vous définissez avec le business les droits et accès standards accordés aux utilisateurs (en fonction de leur rôle/profil).
Vous préparez les supports de formation (en collaboration avec le BPA) et assurez la formation des utilisateurs finaux.
Vous êtes le premier garant de la qualité des livrables dont vous avez la responsabilité ou sur lesquels vous êtes impliqués :
FDD
Source-to-Target Mapping
EDWH and BI Logical Data Model
SAS VA Reports
Tests & Issue Log
Training material
Vous supportez le Project Manager et le Lead Fonctionnel dans leur prise de décision concernant la conduite du projet, les risques identifiés et les solutions pour les mitiger, le scope à développer et l’effort nécessaire à l’exécution des tâches.



VOTRE PROFIL


Formation : Vous avez un master à orientation économique, IT ou statistique.
Expérience : Vous avez une expérience de minimum 5 ans en Business Intelligence et Datawarehouse dans un rôle d’analyste ou de business analyst.
Compétences linguistiques : maîtrise du français ou du néerlandais, avec une bonne connaissance de la deuxième langue ainsi que de l'anglais.
Compétences techniques : maîtrise des Utilities et/ou une expérience réussie dans le domaine d’au moins 3 ans (idéalement en BI), connaissance de SQL (dans le cadre du prototyping nous utilisons l’outil EGuide), connaissance en SAS VA.
Compétences et attitudes clés : orienté résultat & client, communication, forte résistance au stress, esprit analytique, autonome, capacité d’apprentissage, structuré & organisé pour tenir les délais.



NOTRE OFFRE


Une entreprise certifiée Top Employer, valorisant le bien-être et le développement de ses collaborateurs.
Un job porteur de sens, au service de la ville de Bruxelles et de ses habitants, dans un secteur d’activité en perpétuelle évolution.
Une formation de qualité et valorisante, ainsi que des perspectives de carrière.
Un package financier et extralégal avantageux (possibilités de télétravail, voiture de société électrique avec une carte de chargement ou un budget mobilité, GSM, frais internet, allocation de représentation, 33 jours de congé, chèques-repas, écochèques, flex income plan, assurances telles que soins de santé, hospitalisation et pension, 30% de réduction sur la facture d’énergie,…).



Localisation du poste

Localisation du poste

Quai des Usines 16, 1000 Bruxelles

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We're looking for a consultant to join our client's team, taking on a pivotal role in implementing and maintaining high-quality data solutions for anti-money laundering (AML) processes. This position is essential for ensuring compliance, efficiency, and scalability across various operations within the organization. You will bridge the gap between data providers and data consumers, aligning technical solutions with business needs while adhering to strict data governance and quality standards.

Responsibilities

Your core responsibilities will encompass various facets of data management:


Stakeholder Alignment & Communication
Facilitate collaboration among IT, Data Hub, Compliance, and Operations to ensure consistent alignment through structured documentation, workshops, and transparent communication.
Convert complex business requirements into actionable data solutions while balancing technical feasibility with regulatory and operational needs.
Data Governance & Quality
Develop and enforce data governance frameworks, including role definitions and quality rules, in conjunction with data providers and consumers.
Proactively monitor and refine data quality issues to maintain the integrity of AML-related datasets.
Document business and technical rules for data integration to ensure compliance and traceability.
Data Analysis & Troubleshooting
Investigate and resolve data discrepancies, particularly in raw data formats.
Conduct analysis of AML Transaction Monitoring data to identify patterns and gaps affecting compliance and operational efficiency.

Role and Profile

The ideal candidate will possess a blend of technical and business expertise, including:


Technical Experience
5+ years of experience in Data Analytics, particularly within AML processes.
Hands-on proficiency in SQL and data modeling tools.
Experience with reporting and dashboard tools.
Business Expertise
Comprehensive understanding of banking data, especially AML Transaction Monitoring.
Ability to capture and manage functional and non-functional requirements for data solutions.
Knowledge of data security and IT architecture within financial services.

Soft Skills And Language Requirements

The successful candidate will demonstrate strong interpersonal skills and a proactive mindset:


Excellent communication skills in Dutch, French, and English to engage with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Proactive, analytical, and problem-solving abilities, with extreme attention to detail and a focus on quality.
Adaptability and a willingness to learn, effectively linking strategic objectives to operational execution.



A Master’s degree in a quantitative discipline is preferred, though candidates with relevant analytics knowledge in other fields will also be considered. The role will involve a hybrid working model, balancing onsite and home working equally.

Contactperson & Reference


Reference #: INW25874
Jeroen Van Hoecke
jeroen.vanhoecke@i4m.be

Title: Data Analyst (Insurance Sector)
Description:
For an innovative organization active within the insurance and financial services sector, we are looking for a Data Analyst. In this position, you will play a key role in transforming complex data into actionable insights that support strategic and operational decision-making across the business.

You will work in a data-driven environment where analytics, reporting, and continuous improvement are central. The role combines technical expertise with strong stakeholder interaction and offers the opportunity to contribute to both business performance and process optimization.



Your Responsibilities



Data Management & Preparation


Collect and consolidate data from multiple internal and external sources
Clean, validate, and structure datasets to ensure data quality and consistency
Support the maintenance and optimization of reporting datasets and analytical environments


Data Analysis & Insights


Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and business opportunities
Perform statistical and exploratory data analyses
Translate complex data into clear business insights and recommendations
Support business teams with ad hoc analyses and reporting requests


Reporting & Visualization


Develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations using modern BI tools
Present data in a clear and business-friendly way for both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Monitor KPIs and business performance metrics


Stakeholder Collaboration


Work closely with business, operational, and IT teams to understand reporting and analytical needs
Support decision-making through data-driven recommendations
Participate in initiatives focused on process improvement and data quality enhancement


Continuous Improvement


Stay informed about new technologies, tools, and best practices in analytics and BI
Contribute to the evolution of the organization's data capabilities and reporting standards
Share knowledge and collaborate within the broader data community


Your Profile


Experience in Data Analysis, Reporting, BI, or a similar analytical role
Strong knowledge of SQL for querying and manipulating data
Experience with reporting and visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar
Familiarity with Python or R is considered a strong asset
Understanding of data modeling and data warehousing concepts
Strong analytical mindset with excellent problem-solving skills
Able to communicate complex insights in a clear and structured way
Autonomous, detail-oriented, and proactive
Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant field such as Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or similar
Professional level of English and fluent in either Dutch or French


What's Offered


The opportunity to join a modern and data-driven organization
A collaborative environment where innovation and analytics are highly valued
Exposure to large-scale data and impactful business projects
Flexibility, autonomy, and opportunities for professional growth
Competitive salary package with additional benefits
